title: NOC 41200 – University Professors and Lecturers | GTR Immigration
description: University Professors and Lecturers under NOC 41200 are senior academic professionals responsible for teaching, research, and academic leadership within Canada’s post-secondary institutions. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role requires advanced academic credentials and contributes directly to Canada’s education and innovation sectors. These professionals work in universities, colleges, teacher-training institutions, and research-intensive academic environments. They play a critical role in shaping future professionals, advancing research, and strengthening Canada’s global academic reputation.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group412 – Professional occupations in education servicesMinor Group4120 – University professors and post-secondary assistantsNOC Code41200

duties: University Professors and Lecturers perform some or all of the following duties:
Prepare and deliver lectures and laboratory sessions
Advise students on academic and career matters
Conduct research and publish scholarly work
Serve on academic committees (curriculum, admissions, degree requirements)
Provide professional consultancy services to government and industry
employment_requirements: University Professors: Doctoral degree (PhD) in field of specialization
University Lecturers: Master’s degree in field of specialization
Professional certification for regulated disciplines (medicine, engineering, law, psychology, architecture)
Postdoctoral research experience (preferred for research-intensive universities)
salary_prospects: University Professors and Lecturers typically earn between:
$41,100 – $155,500 per year, depending on specialization, tenure status, research output, and institution.
Senior professors, department chairs, and research faculty may earn significantly higher compensation.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41200 remains balanced and stable due to:
Growth in STEM and applied sciences programs
Government investment in research and innovation
Competition can be strong for tenure-track roles at major research universities.
labour_market_demand: University Professors and Lecturers are employed across:
Development of emerging fields (AI, healthcare, sustainability, engineering)
Opportunities are concentrated in provinces such as Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta.
Canada’s multicultural academic environment makes it an attractive destination for international scholars and educators.
